description: Full integration of the upgraded ESP32 auto-tune system into the rustRoast backend and SvelteKit dashboard. Extends the backend start payload to forward new configurable parameters (mode, tuning method, relay params, aggressiveness), updates frontend types/store/API client for both relay and step response modes, adds mode/method selectors and advanced parameter inputs to the AutotunePanel UI, displays quality metrics in results, and updates the compact dashboard widget.

userStories.title: Extend backend AutoTuneStartPayload to forward all configurable parameters
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I want the /api/roaster/{device_id}/autotune/start endpoint to accept and forward all new auto-tune parameters (mode, tuning_method, bias, amplitude, hysteresis, aggressiveness) to the ESP32 via MQTT, so the dashboard can control the full auto-tune configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: In crates/server/src/main.rs, extend AutoTuneStartPayload struct with optional fields: mode (Option<String>), tuning_method (Option<String>), bias (Option<f64>), amplitude (Option<f64>), hysteresis (Option<f64>), aggressiveness (Option<f64>) — all with #[serde(default)]
In api_autotune_start(), construct the MQTT JSON payload by serializing the full AutoTuneStartPayload body (using serde_json::to_string(&body)) instead of hardcoding only target_temperature
Add #[derive(Serialize)] to AutoTuneStartPayload so it can be serialized for MQTT

Add validation: if mode is provided, must be 'relay' or 'step_response'; if tuning_method provided, must be one of 'zn_classic', 'tyreus_luyben', 'zn_some_overshoot', 'zn_no_overshoot'; if bias provided, must be 10.0..=90.0; if amplitude provided, must be 5.0..=45.0; if hysteresis provided, must be 0.1..=5.0; if aggressiveness provided, must be 0.1..=2.0
Return 400 Bad Request with descriptive message for any validation failure
Backward compatible: existing calls with only target_temperature still work (all new fields are Option with serde default)
Add #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] to all optional fields so the MQTT JSON only includes fields that were actually provided
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update frontend TypeScript types for auto-tune modes and quality metrics
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want structured TypeScript interfaces for the new auto-tune parameters, status phases, and result quality metrics so the store and UI components have type-safe access to all fields.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/dashboard/src/lib/types/telemetry.ts, update AutotuneWsMessage.autotune.data from Record<string, unknown> to a union type or keep Record<string, unknown> but add a comment documenting the expected fields for both status and results
In apps/dashboard/src/lib/stores/autotune.svelte.ts, extend AutotuneStatus interface: add optional mode (string), totalSteps (number) fields

In apps/dashboard/src/lib/stores/autotune.svelte.ts, extend AutotuneResults interface with: mode (optional string), tuning_method (optional string), quality (optional string — 'good'|'acceptable'|'fallback'|'poor'), original_kp/ki/kd (optional numbers), oscillation_period (optional number), oscillation_amplitude (optional number), ultimate_gain (optional number), consistency_pct (optional number), asymmetry_ratio (optional number), hysteresis_correction (optional number), process_gain_K (optional number), time_constant_tau (optional number), dead_time_theta (optional number), aggressiveness (optional number), simc_tau_c (optional number), duration (optional number)

Add a new exported interface AutotuneStartParams: { targetTemp: number; mode?: 'relay' | 'step_response'; tuningMethod?: string; bias?: number; amplitude?: number; hysteresis?: number; aggressiveness?: number; }
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update frontend API client to pass auto-tune start parameters and expose history
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the autotune API client to accept and send all configurable parameters on start, and expose the history endpoints that already exist in the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/dashboard/src/lib/api/client.ts, update autotune.start() signature to accept a params object (AutotuneStartParams) instead of just (deviceId, targetTemp)

autotune.start() sends JSON body with target_temperature plus any provided optional fields: mode, tuning_method, bias, amplitude, hysteresis, aggressiveness — using snake_case keys matching the backend struct
Import or define AutotuneStartParams type in client.ts (import from stores/autotune.svelte.ts or define locally)
Update AutotuneResultsResponse interface: change results field from { Kp: number; Ki: number; Kd: number } to Record<string, unknown> (the store handles typed parsing)
Add autotune.getStatusHistory(deviceId, sinceSecs?, limit?) method calling GET /api/roaster/{deviceId}/autotune/status with query params
Add autotune.getResultsHistory(deviceId, sinceSecs?, limit?) method calling GET /api/roaster/{deviceId}/autotune/results with query params
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update autotune store for new parameters, step response phases, and quality metrics
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the autotune reactive store to handle the new auto-tune modes, forward configurable parameters on start, parse quality metrics from results, and recognize step response phase names.
acceptanceCriteria: Update startAutotune() signature to accept AutotuneStartParams instead of (deviceId, targetTemp)
startAutotune() passes the full params object to autotuneApi.start()
Store the selected mode in autotuneState (add mode field to the $state object, default 'relay')
In handleAutotuneEvent() for status messages: recognize step response phases — add 'STEP_BASELINE', 'STEP_UP', 'STEP_SETTLE', 'STEP_ANALYZE' to activePhases array
In handleAutotuneEvent() for status messages: parse 'mode' field from data and store in autotuneState.status.mode

In handleAutotuneEvent() for status messages: parse 'total_steps' field and use it for progress calculation instead of hardcoded 15; if not present, default to 12 for relay or use phase-based progress for step response

In handleAutotuneEvent() for results messages: parse all new quality metric fields into the extended AutotuneResults interface (tuning_method, quality, oscillation_period, oscillation_amplitude, ultimate_gain, consistency_pct, asymmetry_ratio, hysteresis_correction, process_gain_K, time_constant_tau, dead_time_theta, aggressiveness, simc_tau_c, mode, duration, original_kp/ki/kd)
In fetchLatestAutotune(): parse and store the extended results fields on page reload recovery
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add mode selector, tuning method selector, and advanced parameters to AutotunePanel
userStories.description: As a roaster operator, I want to choose between relay and step response auto-tune modes, select a tuning method, and optionally configure advanced relay parameters before starting auto-tune.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a mode selector below the target temperature input: two radio buttons or a select dropdown with options 'Relay (Oscillation)' and 'Step Response (SIMC)' — default to 'relay'
When mode is 'relay', show a tuning method dropdown below mode selector: 'Tyreus-Luyben (Conservative)' (default), 'Z-N Classic (Aggressive)', 'Z-N Some Overshoot', 'Z-N No Overshoot'
When mode is 'step_response', show an aggressiveness slider or number input (0.1 to 2.0, default 1.0) with label 'Aggressiveness' and help text 'Lower = more conservative, Higher = faster response'
Add a collapsible 'Advanced Parameters' section (collapsed by default) with inputs for: Bias (10-90%, default 50), Amplitude (5-45%, default 25), Hysteresis (0.1-5.0°C, default 1.0)
All selector values and parameter inputs are disabled during active auto-tune (autotuneState.isAutotuning)
handleStart() passes all configured parameters to startAutotune() using the AutotuneStartParams interface
Input styling matches existing target temperature input: same border, bg, focus ring classes
Step count display during RUNNING phase shows 'Step X of 12' (from totalSteps) instead of hardcoded '~15'
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add quality metrics display and step response phase support to AutotunePanel results
userStories.description: As a roaster operator, I want to see auto-tune quality metrics and step response phase indicators so I can evaluate the tuning confidence before applying results.
acceptanceCriteria: Add step response phase colors and labels: STEP_BASELINE = 'Baseline' (text-cyan-400), STEP_UP = 'Step Up' (text-amber-400), STEP_SETTLE = 'Settling' (text-blue-400), STEP_ANALYZE = 'Analyzing' (text-blue-400)
Add phase color for FAILED state: text-red-400, bg-red-500 (currently only ERROR is handled)
In results section, show a quality badge above the PID table: 'Good' (green), 'Acceptable' (amber), 'Poor' (red), 'Fallback' (gray) based on autotuneState.results.quality
Show the tuning method used: e.g., 'Method: Tyreus-Luyben' or 'Method: SIMC Step Response' below the quality badge
For relay mode results, show a collapsible 'Tuning Details' section with: Oscillation Period (seconds), Oscillation Amplitude (°C), Ultimate Gain (Ku), Consistency (%), Asymmetry Ratio
For step_response mode results, show a collapsible 'Process Model (FOPDT)' section with: Process Gain K, Time Constant τ (seconds), Dead Time θ (seconds), Aggressiveness, Closed-loop τc (seconds)
Show duration of auto-tune run if available: 'Completed in Xm Ys'
Show original PID values in the comparison table (from results.original_kp/ki/kd) instead of reading from live telemetry — the original values are the ones from before auto-tune started
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update AutotuneDashboardWidget for step response modes and quality indicator
userStories.description: As a roaster operator viewing the dashboard, I want the compact autotune widget to show the correct phase labels, mode indicator, and quality badge for both relay and step response modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add step response phase labels and colors matching AutotunePanel: STEP_BASELINE, STEP_UP, STEP_SETTLE, STEP_ANALYZE with appropriate colors
Add FAILED phase handling (same as ERROR — text-red-400)
When auto-tune is active, show a small mode indicator: 'Relay' or 'Step' next to the phase label
In results display, show quality badge inline: small colored dot or text (Good/Acceptable/Poor/Fallback) next to 'Recommended PID' heading
Step count display uses totalSteps from store instead of hardcoded 15
Progress calculation uses totalSteps from store instead of hardcoded 15
All changes maintain the compact widget layout (p-3 spacing, smaller text)
npm run check passes (typecheck)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update autotune tests for new modes, parameters, and quality metrics
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive vitest tests covering the new auto-tune functionality to prevent regressions.
acceptanceCriteria: In apps/dashboard/src/lib/stores/autotune.test.ts, add test: 'parses step response status phases' — verify STEP_BASELINE, STEP_UP, STEP_SETTLE, STEP_ANALYZE are recognized as active phases with isAutotuning = true
Add test: 'parses FAILED phase as inactive' — verify FAILED sets isAutotuning = false
Add test: 'parses extended results with quality metrics' — verify tuning_method, quality, oscillation_period, consistency_pct, mode fields are parsed from results event data
Add test: 'parses step response results with FOPDT model' — verify process_gain_K, time_constant_tau, dead_time_theta, aggressiveness, simc_tau_c are parsed
Add test: 'uses total_steps for progress calculation' — verify progress uses total_steps from status data instead of hardcoded 15
Add test: 'parses mode from status messages' — verify mode field is stored in autotuneState.status
Add test: 'startAutotune passes full params' — mock fetch and verify all AutotuneStartParams fields are sent in the request body with correct snake_case keys
All existing tests still pass
npm test passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Final audit: verify full stack integration compiles, typechecks, and tests pass
userStories.description: As the project owner, I want a comprehensive audit confirming that all rustRoast changes compile, typecheck, pass tests, and integrate correctly with the ESP32 auto-tune MQTT protocol.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo build --workspace succeeds with no errors
cargo test --workspace passes all tests
cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings passes with no warnings
cd apps/dashboard && npm run check passes (SvelteKit typecheck)
cd apps/dashboard && npm test passes all vitest tests
Verify AutoTuneStartPayload in main.rs has all 7 fields (target_temperature + 6 optional) with correct serde attributes
Verify api_autotune_start forwards the full serialized body as MQTT payload
Verify AutotuneResults interface in autotune.svelte.ts has all quality metric fields for both relay and step response modes
Verify AutotuneStartParams interface exists and is used by startAutotune() and autotune.start()
Verify AutotunePanel.svelte has mode selector, tuning method selector, advanced parameters section, and quality metrics display
Verify AutotuneDashboardWidget.svelte has step response phase support and quality badge
Verify handleAutotuneEvent() recognizes all step response phases as active
Verify backward compatibility: a start request with only target_temperature still works
If any issue is found, fix it now and document what was fixed
Typecheck passes
